assertions.evidence: @TableTest(""" ... """) is present at the start of the code block

assertions.text: Table uses value-set syntax (curly braces like {READ, WRITE} or {READ, WRITE, DELETE}) to express rules that hold across multiple actions — rather than one row per role/action combination
assertions.evidence: ADMIN row uses {READ, WRITE, DELETE} and GUEST row uses {WRITE, DELETE}

assertions.text: Table has fewer than 9 data rows (i.e. does not enumerate every role/action combination individually)
assertions.evidence: The table has 6 data rows: ADMIN can do anything, USER can read, USER can write, USER cannot delete, GUEST can read, GUEST cannot write

assertions.text: Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: Test method body only contains: assertEquals(canPerform, permissions.canPerform(role, action));

assertions.text: Table has a scenario/description column as the leftmost column
assertions.evidence: The leftmost column is 'Scenario' with values like 'ADMIN can do anything', 'USER can read', etc.

assertions.text: No two rows share both the same Role value and the same boolean output — forces value set consolidation (e.g. USER can READ and USER can WRITE, both true, should be one row with {READ, WRITE}).
assertions.evidence: USER rows: 'USER can read' (USER, READ, true) and 'USER can write' (USER, WRITE, true) — both share Role=USER and output=true, but are listed as separate rows instead of being consolidated into one row with {READ, WRITE}

assertions.text: Test method has either a @DisplayName annotation or a method name that reads as a clear, descriptive title when converted from camelCase/snake_case
assertions.evidence: Method name is 'permissionCheck' which converts to 'Permission Check' — descriptive and not generic

assertions.text: @Description is present and provides context beyond what the table rows already express — such as where permissions are checked in the request lifecycle, or open questions. Does NOT merely restate the column names or summarise what the rows show.
assertions.evidence: No @Description annotation is present in the code output at all

assertions.text: Annotations appear in order: @DisplayName (if present), @Description (if present), @TableTest — not any other order.
assertions.evidence: Only @TableTest is present; no @DisplayName or @Description annotations exist, so ordering constraint is trivially satisfied

assertions.text: @Description text uses a text block (triple-quoted string """) when the text is long, not string concatenation with +.
assertions.evidence: No @Description annotation is present in the code output, so this assertion cannot be satisfied
